name: Keboola Data Platform API
what_it_does: Data platform with comprehensive REST API — enabling data teams to orchestrate complete data pipelines (extraction, transformation, loading, reverse ETL) with a component-based architecture, supporting 400+ pre-built extractors and writers, Python/SQL/R transformations, and multi-cloud deployments, with strong programmatic API for DataOps automation.
use_cases: Orchestrating data pipelines via Keboola API for end-to-end ELT automation
Running transformations via Keboola API for SQL/Python/R data processing jobs
Managing storage tables via Keboola Storage API for data warehouse table operations
Triggering component jobs via Keboola API for event-driven data pipeline execution
Building DataOps workflows via Keboola API for automated data platform management
not_for: Real-time streaming (Keboola is primarily batch-oriented ETL/ELT)
Simple one-source-one-destination pipelines (use Fivetran/Airbyte for simpler setups)
Teams needing open-source self-hosted only (Keboola is SaaS-primary)
best_when: A data engineering team needs a comprehensive data platform with programmatic API control — DataOps teams automating pipeline management, transformation orchestration, and data operations at scale.
avoid_when: You need simple source-to-destination ELT without orchestration complexity, or real-time streaming.

auth.notes: Storage API token: X-StorageApi-Token header. Master token (admin) vs project tokens (scoped). Manage API: separate manage.keboola.com API for organization management with Master token. Storage API: connection.{region}.keboola.com/v2/storage/. Component runner: connection.{region}.keboola.com/v2/run/. Python SDK: keboola.component library. Region-specific: US East, EU, US West, etc. Token scopes: read-only, bucket-limited tokens available.

pricing.notes: Czech company (Prague). Founded 2012 by Pavel Doležal and Milan Veverka. Keboola Connection: core platform. Region deployments: US, EU, Azure EU, GCP. Components: 400+ extractors, transformers, writers. Storage: proprietary Keboola Storage (S3/GCS/Azure backed). Transformations: SQL (Snowflake/BigQuery native), Python sandbox, R sandbox, Julia. Orchestrations: workflow orchestration for component chaining. Data Catalog: internal catalog for project data assets. Telemetry: built-in pipeline monitoring. Good DataOps API: strong REST API for programmatic control. Used by: large European enterprises, e-commerce, finance.

performance.rate_limits: 50 concurrent jobs; API rate limits per token

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: X-StorageApi-Token header. Master vs project tokens (scope difference). Region-specific API endpoints. Separate Manage API for org management. Python SDK. Czech company. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR. Annual contracts. 400+ components.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Storage table reads idempotent. Component jobs: job IDs are unique — check job status before retrying. Table imports: idempotent with incremental/full load modes. Orchestration runs: check if already running before triggering.
known_agent_gotchas: X-StorageApi-Token: use project token (not Master token) for project-scoped operations
Region-specific endpoints: connection.{region}.keboola.com — use correct region for your project
Master token vs project token: different APIs and permissions — document which is needed per operation
Separate Manage API: organization-level operations use manage.keboola.com with different auth
Component jobs are async: trigger job → poll for completion → retrieve output
Storage table names must follow Keboola naming conventions (no special chars except underscore)

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. X-StorageApi-Token: scoped project tokens with bucket-level restrictions. SOC2/ISO27001/GDPR certified. Czech company: strong GDPR compliance (EU headquarters). Data at rest: encrypted in Keboola Storage. Source credentials stored in Keboola — managed secret storage. Master token: high-privilege — restrict to admin use only. Region isolation: EU data stays in EU. Token rotation: generate new project tokens regularly.
